rulings.Case Title: ISHIDEN 6, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. THE BULKLEY FAMILY LIMITED PARTNERSHIP; A ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Special Motion To Strike Pursuant To Ccp 425.16
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT V DOROTHEA LLC, AN OREGON LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CCP 425.16 is CONTINUED to May 21, 2025 at 9:30 a.m., on the Court's own motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LALA HAKOBYAN ET AL VS. ALEKSANDR GEYLER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs References To And Requests For Punitive Damages; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 9, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT ALEKSANDR GEYLER, ALLA TEPER NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFFS REFERENCES TO AND REQUESTS FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES (MOTIONS) is DENIED. The Complaint alleges sufficient facts showing that the conduct was intended by the defendants to cause injury to the plaintiffs, which is all that is required under Civil Code 3294 (a). (See Complaint, paragraph 23, 27- 32, 36, 42-43.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
